mission.title: Install and Configure Cartography on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cartography is a Python tool that consolidates infrastructure assets and their relationships in an intuitive graph view powered by a Neo4j database. Installing Cartography allows you to explore and automate security scenarios, helping to identify risks and improve security posture.

steps.title: Add the Cartography Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Cartography Helm repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add cartography https://cartography-cncf.github.io/cartography/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Cartography using Helm
steps.description:
Install Cartography in a new namespace called 'cartography':
```bash
helm install cartography cartography/cartography --namespace cartography --create-namespace --version 0.130.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Cartography pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cartography
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Cartography deployment to set resource limits:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment cartography --namespace cartography --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access the Cartography web interface
steps.description:
Port-forward the Cartography service to access the web interface:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/cartography --namespace cartography 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Cartography pods running and accessible via the web interface at http://localhost:8080. You should also see the resource limits applied to the deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cartography https://cartography-cncf.github.io/cartography/charts
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment cartography --namespace cartography --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
